generated_from.scan_command: git ls-tree -r --name-only HEAD | filter *.rs plus syntax scan for allow(unsafe_code), unsafe blocks, unsafe fn, unsafe impl, unsafe trait; comments and string literals ignored for unsafe syntax matches
notes: The repository had peer-owned dirty source files at generation time; this ledger inventories committed HEAD so the artifact is reproducible from the source revision recorded here.
UNSAFE-2 catch-up refreshed operation_locators to exact committed-source line numbers and added rows for src/bin/atp.rs and tests/net_unix_stream_ancillary.rs.
Passing the contract proves source/ledger locator alignment only; category evidence still controls any safety claim.

categories.reactor-ffi: Linux/macOS/Windows reactor FFI and raw OS handle management.
categories.filesystem-ffi: Filesystem raw-fd or platform-specific I/O boundary.
categories.process-signal-ffi: Process, signal, wait, PID, process-group, or platform event handle boundary.
categories.env-var-mutation: Process-global environment mutation in tests or configuration setup.
categories.platform-resource-metrics: OS resource usage, load, handle-count, or socket-table metrics APIs.
categories.simd-kernel: Target-feature-gated SIMD kernels for GF(256)/RaptorQ paths.
categories.sync-internal-cell: Interior-mutability primitives or manual Send/Sync implementations.
categories.network-ffi: Network socket and raw stream boundary.
categories.compat-boundary: Satellite compatibility crate boundary, outside core runtime production graph.
categories.conformance-boundary: Conformance binary/harness boundary, outside production runtime proof.
categories.fuzz-target: Fuzz harness boundary, outside production runtime proof.
categories.test-or-lab-harness: Test-only or deterministic lab fixture boundary.
categories.observability-test-boundary: Observability test or intentionally invalid data construction boundary.
categories.atp-platform-boundary: ATP platform capability or journal probe boundary.
categories.other: Unclassified boundary requiring follow-up before enforcement closes.

required_evidence: RCH cargo check or test lane for the target-gated reactor module.
Focused reactor tests for registration, rearm, stale-token, and close/drop behavior.
Explicit host coverage notes for platform-specific paths that were not executed.
review_workflow: Prefer item-scope #[allow(unsafe_code)] for narrow FFI calls; file-scope allows must name contained operations.
Verify raw descriptors or handles cannot be double-closed, reused after deregistration, or observed through stale tokens.
explicit_no_claims: Passing the ledger contract does not execute platform FFI.
Linux proof does not prove macOS or Windows reactor behavior.

required_evidence: Focused filesystem or descriptor tests for ownership, drop, and error-path cleanup.
Assertions that stale, duplicated, or invalid handles are rejected or cleaned up deterministically.
review_workflow: Trace every unsafe descriptor conversion to a single owner.
Confirm cleanup paths are cancel-safe and do not rely on ambient process state.
explicit_no_claims: Filesystem FFI evidence does not prove reactor readiness semantics.
Host-specific descriptor tests do not cover every platform.

required_evidence: Process lifecycle tests that tolerate exit races and verify cleanup.
Platform notes for PID, process group, wait, signal, or Windows event behavior.
review_workflow: Verify signal or process operations are bounded to owned children or documented probes.
Document any unavoidable global process-state assumptions.
explicit_no_claims: Process-signal evidence does not prove general scheduler cancellation.
Unix signal proof does not prove Windows event behavior.

required_evidence: Serialized setup/restore proof or test isolation evidence.
A no-production-safety claim for process-global environment mutation.
review_workflow: Keep environment mutation helpers scoped to tests or explicit configuration setup.
Verify every mutation path restores or removes prior state deterministically.
explicit_no_claims: Environment mutation evidence is not production runtime safety proof.
Passing env-var tests does not prove concurrent process-global mutation is safe.

required_evidence: Focused parser or probe tests for OS resource, load, handle-count, or socket-table APIs.
Bounds checks for kernel-returned sizes and partial records.
review_workflow: Treat kernel metrics as untrusted inputs.
Verify malformed or truncated platform data degrades without panics.
explicit_no_claims: Metrics evidence does not prove admission or scheduling correctness.
One host's resource API proof does not cover every OS version.

required_evidence: Scalar parity tests for every SIMD path.
Feature-gate proof for target-specific instruction use.
Architecture-specific RCH evidence or an explicit no-host boundary.
review_workflow: Confirm unsafe intrinsics never run without the required target feature.
Compare SIMD output against the scalar implementation for representative and boundary inputs.
explicit_no_claims: Scalar parity does not prove all CPU microarchitectures.
Feature-gated SIMD proof does not cover disabled-feature fallback unless separately tested.

required_evidence: Loom, lab, or deterministic concurrency tests for aliasing, drop, and wake protocol invariants.
A documented Send/Sync or interior-mutability safety invariant.
review_workflow: Review aliasing, lifetime, and drop ordering before performance claims.
Tie every manual Send/Sync assertion to the owning synchronization protocol.
explicit_no_claims: A local contract test does not prove every scheduler interleaving.
Manual Send/Sync evidence does not transfer to unrelated types.

required_evidence: Tests that prove the unsafe exists for security semantics such as zeroization or constant-time handling.
Reference-validity checks for any raw pointer or uninitialized-memory handling.
review_workflow: Keep the security property and memory-safety invariant separate in review.
Verify unsafe code does not expose invalid references while enforcing the security behavior.
explicit_no_claims: Secret-memory tests do not prove broad cryptographic security.
Zeroization evidence does not prove scheduler or I/O safety.

required_evidence: Test-only confinement proof or focused database FFI/protocol tests.
Sanitization or rejection assertions for intentionally invalid data.
review_workflow: Confirm the unsafe boundary cannot leak into default production database paths without a new row.
Document whether the row is a test fixture, protocol audit, or runtime FFI boundary.
explicit_no_claims: Database test unsafe does not prove production database driver safety.
Invalid-data construction evidence is not a correctness proof for the full protocol.

required_evidence: Socket ownership and nonblocking setup tests.
Cancellation or drop-path checks for raw streams and socket handles.
review_workflow: Verify socket state transitions are explicit and cancel-safe.
Keep raw handle ownership local to the smallest boundary that needs it.
explicit_no_claims: Network FFI tests do not prove HTTP, TLS, or transport-layer semantics.
One address-family proof does not cover every socket family.

required_evidence: Wasm or browser-boundary tests, or a documented host limitation.
Explicit distinction between Rust browser preview surfaces and JS/TS Browser Edition GA claims.
review_workflow: Check that browser unsafe rows remain inside wasm/browser interop boundaries.
Do not cite browser-boundary rows as native runtime proof.
explicit_no_claims: Browser-boundary evidence does not prove native reactor behavior.
Preview Rust browser surfaces are not GA support claims.

required_evidence: Opt-in compatibility crate tests for the adapter surface.
A no-core-runtime-production-claim boundary for tokio/hyper-shaped wrappers.
review_workflow: Keep compat unsafe outside the core runtime default feature graph.
Verify adapter unsafe does not weaken the no-Tokio production guarantee.
explicit_no_claims: Compat evidence does not prove core runtime production safety.
Compat crate dependencies are not default asupersync runtime dependencies.

required_evidence: Conformance-only test or vendor-comparison evidence.
A no-production-proof claim for helper unsafe used by the conformance harness.
review_workflow: Keep vendor comparison helpers scoped away from runtime API guarantees.
Document any process-global or platform-specific mutation used by conformance tests.
explicit_no_claims: Conformance harness unsafe does not prove default production runtime safety.
Vendor comparison evidence does not prove asupersync implementation correctness by itself.

required_evidence: Fuzz harness build or smoke evidence.
Proof that harness-only unsafe remains outside production runtime proof.
review_workflow: Keep raw wakers, pinning helpers, and allocator hooks confined to fuzz targets.
Do not reuse fuzz-only unsafe helpers in production modules without a new category row.
explicit_no_claims: Fuzz harness evidence is not a release-readiness claim.
A fuzz smoke run does not prove absence of undefined behavior.

required_evidence: Focused unit or lab tests for the fixture that uses unsafe.
A no-production-API claim for raw-waker or deterministic fixture helpers.
review_workflow: Keep test-only unsafe out of exported runtime API paths.
Verify fixture unsafe cannot mask task, obligation, or region leaks.
explicit_no_claims: Test harness unsafe does not prove production runtime safety.
Raw-waker fixture evidence does not prove executor waker correctness globally.

required_evidence: Single-owner and drop-invariant tests for channel fixtures.
Cancellation-path assertions for any extracted channel state.
review_workflow: Confirm the unsafe exists only to inspect or drive test fixtures.
Verify the fixture cannot create duplicate ownership of channel state.
explicit_no_claims: Channel fixture evidence does not prove all channel implementations.
Test extraction proof is not production API proof.

required_evidence: Pin and ownership assertions in the stream fixture test.
A no-production-API claim for any test-only state access.
review_workflow: Verify pinned stream state is not moved after being exposed to unsafe test code.
Keep fixture-only access out of public stream APIs.
explicit_no_claims: Stream fixture evidence does not prove every stream combinator.
Pin fixture proof does not cover unrelated unsafe pinning.

required_evidence: Sanitization or rejection tests for intentionally invalid telemetry/resource data.
A no-production-metrics-safety claim for invalid-data construction.
review_workflow: Keep invalid telemetry construction inside tests or fixtures.
Verify observability code degrades without panics on malformed input.
explicit_no_claims: Observability fixture evidence does not prove metrics backend correctness.
Invalid-data tests do not prove production resource accounting.

required_evidence: ATP platform capability or journal probe tests under RCH, or explicit no-host coverage.
Clear feature/platform gate notes for each host-specific operation.
review_workflow: Verify ATP unsafe remains behind platform capability checks.
Record host coverage separately from protocol-level ATP claims.
explicit_no_claims: ATP platform evidence does not prove the full transfer protocol.
One host proof does not cover every ATP platform boundary.

required_evidence: Reclassify the row before claiming closure.
Open a follow-up blocker if the category cannot be identified.
review_workflow: Do not accept new rows in this category for completed unsafe-review work.
Treat any remaining row as a fail-closed review blocker.
explicit_no_claims: No safety, production, platform, or release claim may cite an other-category row.
